element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Starting equipment; establishes damage model (hit = lose armor, second hit = death)
First enemy type; surrounds Arthur to teach evasion and timing
Series-first mobility; allows mid-air direction change, essential for platforming

element_introduction_order.element: Alternative Weapons (knife, hatchets, crossbows, daggers, torches)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Found during progression; single-slot limitation forces tactical switching
Contain weapon/armor upgrades or deadly traps; Conjurer transformation penalty
Provides Weapon of Enchantment upgrade; intermediate upgrade tier
Enchanted weapon + Moon Shield + charged magic; highest defense tier
Gate-keepers; must be defeated to progress; time-limited encounters
Required to defeat final boss Sardius; found in second playthrough only

typical_relief_triggers: Defeating Foul Guardian and advancing to next level
Passing through safe screen areas without environmental hazard
typical_tension_triggers: Time limit pressure before Foul Guardian encounter
Environmental hazard activation (earthquake, blizzard, avalanche, wave)
Multiple enemy encounters (Skull Flowers, Belials, Fire Killers, Blue Killers)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating difficulty across eight themed levels with progressive enemy density; early gravestone/ship levels introduce mechanics; mid-game caverns and ice escalate environmental threats; final three castle levels concentrate enemy and boss pressure

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden treasure chests appear only when moving through specific screen areas; rewards spatial exploration and knowledge
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Treasure chests hidden in specific locations contain upgrades (armor, weapons, points) or Conjurer traps; requires deliberate search

difficulty_curve.early_game: Level 1 (The Haunted Graveyard) introduces basic zombies and movement; safe armor starting point allows learning of jump/combat timing
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Levels 2-5 escalate through varied environments (sea, caverns, ice) with increased enemy types (Skull Flowers, Belials, Fire Killers, Werewolves) and environmental hazards (waves, blizzards, avalanches); armor upgrades become critical
difficulty_curve.late_game: Levels 6-8 (castle sequences) concentrate Foul Guardians and flying enemies (Red Arremers, Blue Killers) with limited relief; Level 8 (Throne Room) requires Goddess's Bracelet found in second playthrough
notable_spikes: Level 4-2: Rotating palace scaling sequence (Nebulus-style mechanic)
Final Boss (Sardius): Requires second full playthrough to obtain Goddess's Bracelet; unwinnable without this mandatory weapon discovery

notable_level_patterns.description: Five of eight levels (Levels 1-5) consist of two distinct sections; each section ends with a Foul Guardian boss; creates rhythm of: platforming → boss → platforming → boss before level advancement
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Level 1-1 (Graveyard zombie section) → Level 1-2 (continuation with Belial boss)

notable_level_patterns.description: Early levels (1-5) each feature distinct environment with themed hazards: graveyard (no hazard), abandoned ship (waves), flame cavern (fire), rotating palace (gravity/rotation), ice/snow (blizzards, avalanches)
Final three levels (6-8) transition from environmental stages to Emperor Sardius's castle; increased enemy density, reduced environmental hazards, concentrated boss encounters
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Level 8: The Throne Room (final confrontation area)

notable_level_patterns.description: Game design forces complete replay: first playthrough teaches levels and enemies; second playthrough requires finding Goddess's Bracelet (hidden location not revealed in first run) to defeat final boss; creates meta-progression
